extra.reasoning: The property requires that no input makes rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true only by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), whose condition is the 8-way conjunction (heart_failure AND elderly AND renal_impairment AND anticoagulated AND bradycardia AND g6pd_deficiency AND seizure_history AND prior_gi_bleed); otherwise it defaults to false. rec_drug_b is set true by rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) whenever heart_failure is true; otherwise default false. Critically, heart_failure is one of the conjuncts required for rec_drug_a. Therefore any input that satisfies the rec_drug_a conjunction necessarily has heart_failure=true, which also fires rec_b_from_trigger, forcing rec_drug_b=true. There is no rule that can clear/override either output (each output has a single true-setting rule that beats the default; no conflicting rule exists for these outputs), so priorities and tie-breaking do not save it. Concretely, setting all 8 conjuncts true (and the unrelated qt_prolongation, hepatic_impairment, diabetes false, since they only affect rec_drug_c) yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. The property is violated.
